question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSort()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition lastSerial == DEFAULT_SERIAL_VALUE where DEFAULT_SERIAL_VALUE=0L uses reference equality (==) instead of .equals() for the Long object returned by getSerialNumber(), causing incorrect sort behavior when the Long object is not cached (values outside -128 to 127), leading to pagination errors that expose airdrop records for accounts with unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the path variable {id} as shard.realm.num format with a non-default shard (e.g., 1.0.1234) to bypass account lookup validation, causing the service to query airdrops for an account in a different shard that may have different staking reward rules, enabling cross-shard staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply receiverIds=gt:0.0.0 with limit=1 and repeatedly follow the next-page links to enumerate all outstanding airdrops across the entire network, building a complete map of which accounts have pending airdrop claims that would trigger staking reward payouts, enabling targeted theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TokenAirdropMapper.mapToNullIfZero() method which returns null for serialNumber=0, causing the EXTRACTOR to use DEFAULT_SERIAL_NUMBER='0L' (a string) in the pagination link, which when parsed by NumberRangeParameter.valueOf() is treated as the number 0, creating an infinite pagination loop that repeatedly returns the same fungible token airdrops for accounts with unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user query /api/v1/accounts/{id}/airdrops/pending with no filter parameters and order=asc to retrieve the oldest pending airdrops for an account, identifying long-standing unclaimed airdrops whose claim would trigger large accumulated staking rewards, enabling the attacker to front-run the claim?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound.hasEqualBounds() check in adjustLowerRange() which rewrites a range to EQ when lower==upper, by supplying receiverIds=gte:0.0.X&receiverIds=lte:0.0.X, causing the query to use EQ instead of a range, potentially bypassing index usage and causing a full table scan that leaks timing information about accounts with unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply tokenIds=eq:0.0.X for a specific high-value token to enumerate all accounts that have outstanding airdrops of that token, then cross-reference those accounts with staking reward data to identify which accounts have large unpaid staking rewards that would be triggered upon token claim?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the AirdropRequestType.OUTSTANDING uses TOKEN_AIRDROP.SENDER_ACCOUNT_ID as baseField and TOKEN_AIRDROP.RECEIVER_ACCOUNT_ID as primaryField, while PENDING reverses these, to craft queries that enumerate all sender-receiver pairs and identify accounts with large unpaid staking rewards on both sides of airdrop transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply serialNumbers=gte:1 to filter only NFT airdrops, then enumerate all NFT airdrop senders, and use the sender account IDs to identify accounts with large unpaid staking rewards that would be triggered when the NFT airdrop is claimed, enabling targeted staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound.getInclusiveRangeValue() method which adds 1 to GT values and subtracts 1 from LT values, by supplying serialNumbers=gt:9223372036854775806 (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1), causing an arithmetic overflow when +1 is applied, resulting in a negative serial number that bypasses range validation and returns unintended airdrop records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user query /api/v1/accounts/{id}/airdrops/pending with senderIds=gte:0.0.1&senderIds=lte:0.0.9999999 to enumerate all pending airdrops across a wide range of senders, identifying which sender accounts have large unpaid staking rewards that would be triggered when the pending airdrop is claimed by the victim?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any ownership check in processRequest() to query outstanding airdrops for any account ID, including accounts belonging to validators or node operators with large unpaid staking rewards, and use the airdrop data to time attacks that force staking reward payouts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSort()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the airdrops.getLast() call in getSort() which throws NoSuchElementException on an empty list, but since the isEmpty() check precedes it, craft a concurrent request that causes a race condition where the list becomes empty between the isEmpty() check and getLast() call, causing an unhandled exception that leaks internal state about staking reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the path variable {id} as an EVM address that resolves to the staking reward account (0.0.800), and query its outstanding airdrops to determine which accounts are scheduled to receive staking rewards, enabling the attacker to front-run those reward distributions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply receiverIds with two lte parameters (e.g., lte:0.0.100&lte:0.0.200), causing the Bound constructor to set upper to the last seen upper-bound parameter (lte:0.0.200), silently ignoring the first, and returning more records than the user intended, leaking airdrop data for accounts with unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TokenAirdropRequest.getBounds() logic where if entityIds is empty and tokenIds is empty, only serialNumbers is returned as the bound, causing the DB query to use only serial number as a filter without account ID constraints, potentially returning airdrop records across all accounts and leaking staking reward-related data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getPendingAirdrops() uses PENDING type which sets baseField=RECEIVER_ACCOUNT_ID and primaryField=SENDER_ACCOUNT_ID, to query any account's pending airdrops without authentication, revealing which accounts are about to receive tokens (triggering staking reward payouts) and enabling the attacker to front-run those transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply limit=0 to bypass the @Positive constraint (which requires limit >= 1) if the validation is not enforced at the framework level, causing processRequest() to create a PageRequest with size=0 that returns all records without pagination, leaking complete airdrop data for accounts with unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply receiverIds=ne:0.0.0 to use the NE operator, which is defined in RangeOperator but may not be properly validated in Bound.verifySingleOccurrence() or verifyUnsupported(), causing the DB query to return all records where receiver != 0.0.0, effectively returning all outstanding airdrops across the network and leaking staking reward-related account data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdRangeParameter.valueOf() parsing which splits on ':' and takes the first part as operator and second as entity ID, by supplying a malformed parameter like 'gte:0.0.1:extra' that causes an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException or incorrect parsing, leading to a query that bypasses intended filters and returns unintended airdrop records?
